extraction.one_line_summary: ACKERMANN Pascal won sprint finish in Geneva after BOHLI Tom's late solo attack was caught inside final kilometers
tactical_patterns: BOHLI Tom attacked solo late mimicking prior border tactics, but Bora-hansgrohe remained disciplined and shut down panic
Education First-Drapac attempted opportunistic moves with no sprinter, revealing team willingness to gamble without designated finisher
Peloton acceleration into finale reduced breakaway gap from 90s to 30s in single click, indicating controlled chase not panic

rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: First pro win at 24 after multiple second/third places reveals maturation and sprint finishing power
rider_signals.BOHLI Tom: positive: Strong enough to solo attack in finale despite being domestique, showing late-race opportunism
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Won tour by 8 seconds over BERNAL, maintaining gap through technical finale without incident
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: neutral: Second at 8 seconds confirms climbing strength but reveals inability to gain time on flat finale
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: neutral: Third at 35 seconds, assured podium indicates consistent week but no stage-winning aggression shown
